rag_rules: Use AGREE syntax: assume "label": expression;, guarantee "label": expression;, eq name : type = expression;, const name : type = value;, and assign output = expression;
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations, nested if ... then ... else for branching logic, and AGREE logical operators (and, or, not, =>) for contracts
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type scopes only when concrete bounds, named constants, or visible relations are available
Write output dependency guarantees relating visible outputs to visible inputs in component type scopes
generic_rules: Use concrete bounds, named constants, or visible relations for assumptions and guarantees
Avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts
Use AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) for declarations
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations
Use nested if ... then ... else for branching logic
Use AGREE logical operators (and, or, not, =>) for contracts
anti_patterns: Avoid using AADL property names as AGREE variables
Avoid using invalid AGREE scalar type names (integer, boolean)
Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names are not AGREE variables
